html:
[633]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 院同知镇松江郡州县正官 - Deputy governor or magistrate governing the prefectures, sub-prefectures, and counties of Songjiang.
  • 郡称太守 - Title of the prefectural governor.
  • 州称通守 - Title of the sub-prefectural governor.
  • 县仍曰尹 - Title of the county magistrate.
  • 郡同知称府丞 - Deputy prefectural governor.
  • 知事曰从事 - Title of a subordinate official.
  • 余则损益而已 - The rest are variations or modifications.
  • 秋八月 - The eighth month of autumn.
  • 文炳大举兵 - Wen Bing launched a large-scale military campaign.
  • 悉为所殱 - Were completely annihilated.
  • 仅以身免 - Barely escaped with his life.
  • 士德又与与敬提兵入杭州 - Shi De and Yu Jing led troops into Hangzhou.
  • 军气甚鋭 - The army was very fierce or sharp.
  • 杭州大军敛锋不敌 - The large army of Hangzhou held back and was unable to match them.
  • 丞相退避萧山 - The chancellor retreated to Xiaoshan.
  • 军检刮虏掠 - The army inspected and plundered.
  • 罗木营万户普贤努乃庆元路万户矩珠尔之子 - Luomu Camp Ten Thousand Households Pu Xian Nu, the son of Qinyuan Road Ten Thousand Households Ju Zhu Er.
  • 年未弱冠 - He was not yet in his twenties.
  • 智勇过人 - He was exceptionally intelligent and brave.
  • 率兵先出 - Led his troops out first.
  • 旺札勒图领苖军继进 - Wang Zale Tuling led the Miao army to follow.
  • 民亦挺身巷战 - The people also fought in the streets.
  • 大溃 - Suffered a great defeat.
  • 收拾残兵十丧八九 - Gathered the remaining troops, losing eight or nine out of ten.
  • 又为乍浦钟氏所挠 - Was again hindered by the Zhong clan of Zhapu.
  • 后得马道骁勇 - Later gained a brave and skilled horseman.
  • 禽获苖军无算 - Captured countless Miao soldiers.
  • 西南接境頼此无虞 - The southwestern border relied on this and was free from worry.
  • 不然松江非士诚 - Otherwise, Songjiang would not be under Shi Cheng's control.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 松江 - Refers to Songjiang, a region in present-day Shanghai.
  • 嘉兴 - Refers to Jiaxing, a city in Zhejiang Province.
  • 参政杨旺札勒 - Yang Wang Zale, a high-ranking official.
  • 统领苖獠猺獞 - Leading Miao, Yao, and other ethnic minorities.
  • 名曰达尔罕 - Title 'Darhan,' which might be a transliteration of a foreign or ethnic title.
  • 守御甚坚 - Strong defenses.
  • 屡攻不克 - Repeated unsuccessful attacks.
  • 文炳 - Wen Bing, a military commander.
  • 士德 - Shi De, another military commander.
  • 与敬 - Yu Jing, a military commander.
  • 杭州 - Hangzhou, a major city in Zhejiang Province.
  • 丞相 - The chancellor, a high-ranking government official.
  • 萧山 - Xiaoshan, a district in Hangzhou.
  • 乍浦 - Zhapu, a town in Jiaxing.
  • 钟氏 - The Zhong family, a local influential family.
  • 马道 - Ma Dao, a skilled horseman.
  • 士诚 - Shi Cheng, a military leader.

Proper Nouns

  • 松江 - Songjiang.
  • 嘉兴 - Jiaxing.
  • 参政杨旺札勒 - Yang Wang Zale.
  • 统领苖獠猺獞 - Leading Miao, Yao, and other ethnic minorities.
  • 名曰达尔罕 - Title 'Darhan.'
  • 守御甚坚 - Strong defenses.
  • 屡攻不克 - Repeated unsuccessful attacks.
  • 文炳 - Wen Bing.
  • 士德 - Shi De.
  • 与敬 - Yu Jing.
  • 杭州 - Hangzhou.
  • 丞相 - Chancellor.
  • 萧山 - Xiaoshan.
  • 乍浦 - Zhapu.
  • 钟氏 - Zhong family.
  • 马道 - Ma Dao.
  • 士诚 - Shi Cheng.

[634]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有矣 - Indicates that something has happened or occurred.
  • 托丁氏徃来説合结为婚婣 - Describes the diplomatic efforts of Ding Shi to mediate and form a marital alliance.
  • 昆山之民幸遂苏息 - The people of Kunshan were fortunate to finally find peace and rest.
  • 湖之长兴武康与广德相界 - Describes the geographical location of Changxing, Wuchang, and Guangde, which are adjacent to each other.
  • 花鎗军出没之地 - Refers to the area where the Flower Spear Army operated.
  • 虽互为胜然亦不胜其苦 - Although they alternated in victory and defeat, they still suffered greatly.
  • 所跨三州皆邻勍敌 - The three states they controlled were all neighbors to powerful enemies.
  • 可畏者特集庆一军最盛 - The most formidable enemy was the army of Jiqing, which was the strongest.
  • 陆路则无锡宜兴长兴水路则太湖士马震耀舳舻相衔 - Describes the strength of the forces on land and water.
  • 自后长兴防常州又防士德战败被擒俘致集庆 - Describes the sequence of events leading to the capture of Shide.
  • 俾其作书劝士诚归附 - To make him write a letter to persuade Shicheng to submit.
  • 士德以身徇之终无降意 - Shide sacrificed himself to maintain his resolve, showing no intention to surrender.
  • 士诚势穷力迫愿就丞相招安 - Shicheng, facing dire circumstances and pressure, wished to accept the chancellor's offer of amnesty.
  • 使者徃返讫莫成就 - The envoys made repeated trips but failed to achieve their goal.
  • 仁亲诣江浙省堂具陈自愿休兵息民之意 - Ren personally went to the Jiang-Zhe provincial office to present his willingness to cease hostilities and allow the people to rest.
  • 议始定时十八年秋八月也 - The agreement was finally settled in the eighth month of the eighteenth year of the reign.
  • 朝廷诏赦其罪后授士诚太尉开府平江 - The court issued an edict to pardon his crimes and then appointed him as Taifu and established an office in Pingjiang.
  • 士诚以下授爵有差立江淮分省江浙分枢宻院于平江以设其官属 - Shicheng and those below him were granted different ranks, and the Jianghuai and Jiang-Zhe provincial offices and the Zhongshu Province were established in Pingjiang to set up their officials.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 方国珍 - Fang Guozhen, a prominent figure in the late Yuan and early Ming periods, known for his naval activities.
  • 丁氏 - Ding Shi, a mediator or diplomat involved in forming alliances.
  • 昆山 - Kunshan, a city in Jiangsu Province, known for its strategic importance.
  • 长兴 - Changxing, a county in Zhejiang Province.
  • 武康 - Wuchang, another county in Zhejiang Province.
  • 广德 - Guangde, a county in Anhui Province.
  • 花鎗军 - The Flower Spear Army, a military force known for its unpredictable movements.
  • 集庆 - Jiqing, a city in Jiangsu Province, which was a significant military stronghold.
  • 士德 - Shide, a military leader who was captured and taken to Jiqing.
  • 士诚 - Shicheng, a military leader who eventually accepted the court's offer of amnesty.
  • 丞相 - Chancellor, a high-ranking official in the government.
  • 江浙省堂 - The Jiang-Zhe provincial office, an administrative body in the region.
  • 太尉 - Taifu, a high military rank.
  • 开府 - To establish an office or command post.
  • 平江 - Pingjiang, a city in Jiangsu Province, which became a significant administrative center.

[636]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 飘然有神仙思 - Describes a person who is carefree and has thoughts of immortality or transcendence.
  • 神丹 - Refers to elixirs believed to grant immortality or supernatural powers.
  • 数访公授以其术 - Indicates frequent visits to impart knowledge or skills.
  • 隐去 - Means to disappear or retreat, often used to describe someone who leaves society.
  • 以毒攻毒 - A medical principle meaning to treat poison with poison.
  • 蛊毒犀 - Refers to a type of rhinoceros horn believed to have medicinal properties, particularly in treating poison.
  • 藏公家 - Means 'stored in the family of S,' indicating that the writings were kept within the family.
  • 稍稍传人间 - Means 'gradually spread among people,' suggesting a slow dissemination of information.
  • 虞文靖序其事如此云 - Indicates that the story was written by Yu Wenjing, a historian or scholar.
  • 称地为双 - Refers to a unit of land measurement where '双' means 'pair' or 'two.'
  • 夷有田皆种稻 - Describes the agricultural practices of a certain ethnic group, specifically planting rice.
  • 其佃作三人使二牛前牵中压而后驱之犁 - Describes a specific farming method involving three people and two oxen.
  • 一日为一双 - Means 'one day equals a pair,' referring to the amount of work done in a day.
  • 二己为角 - Means 'two pairs equal a unit,' indicating a larger unit of measurement.
  • 四角为双 - Means 'four units equal a pair,' further defining the measurement system.
  • 约有中原四畆地 - Means 'approximately equal to four mu of land in Central China,' providing a comparison to a standard unit of measurement.
  • 古都国 - Refers to an ancient kingdom or country.
  • 今人讹为骨咄耳 - Indicates that the name has been mispronounced or misunderstood over time.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李玉溪先生 - Refers to a historical figure named Li Yuxi, who was a hermit and alchemist.
  • 赵公琪 - Refers to Zhao Gongqi, a historical figure who was a high-ranking official and a patron of alchemy.
  • 文惠 - A posthumous title given to Zhao Gongqi, indicating his literary and administrative achievements.
  • 临淄 - An ancient city in Shandong Province, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 方士 - Refers to Daoist practitioners or alchemists who sought immortality.
  • 水银硫黄朱砂黄金 - Materials used in alchemy, including mercury, sulfur, cinnabar, and gold.
  • 骨咄犀 - Refers to a type of rhinoceros horn, believed to have medicinal properties.
  • 蛇角 - Refers to the horn of a snake, which is also believed to have medicinal properties.
  • 蛊毒 - Refers to a type of poison or curse, often associated with alchemy and medicine.
  • 唐书 - Refers to the official history of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 金黄华老人 - Refers to an elderly man named Jin Huanghua, a poet.
  • 云南杂志 - Refers to a travelogue or geographical text about Yunnan Province.
  • 夷 - Refers to ethnic minorities or non-Han Chinese groups.

Proper Nouns

  • 李玉溪先生 - Historical figure named Li Yuxi, who was a hermit and alchemist.
  • 赵公琪 - Historical figure who was a high-ranking official and a patron of alchemy.
  • 文惠 - Posthumous title given to Zhao Gongqi, indicating his literary and administrative achievements.
  • 临淄 - Ancient city in Shandong Province, known for its cultural and historical significance.
  • 唐书 - Official history of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 金黄华老人 - Elderly man named Jin Huanghua, a poet.
  • 云南杂志 - Travelogue or geographical text about Yunnan Province.

[637]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一门五节 - Formal expression meaning 'Five Filial Women in One Family.'
  • 一门三节 - Formal expression meaning 'Three Filial Women in One Family.'
  • 以贞节称者 - Means 'known for their chastity and filial piety.'
  • 义不再适 - Means 'refused to remarry out of righteousness.'
  • 引刀截发 - Means 'cut off her hair with a knife' as a vow of chastity.
  • 守志益坚 - Means 'held her resolve even more firmly.'
  • 仕至同知济南路总管府事 - Means 'served as the Deputy Governor of the Total Administration of Jinan Road.'
  • 推恩赠父 - Means 'bestowed posthumous honors on her father.'
  • 封陇西郡夫人 - Means 'bestowed the title of Lady of Longxi County on her mother.'
  • 再调关襄宣慰阶中奉大夫 - Means 'was later promoted to the rank of Middle-Fu Daifu in the Guanxiang Xuanwei Bureau.'
  • 而夫人始卒 - Means 'and the lady finally passed away.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 奉化陈氏 - Refers to the Chen family from Fenghua, Zhejiang.
  • 竺氏 - The first wife of Chen Yuan.
  • 璩氏 - The wife of Chen Dong.
  • 王氏 - The wife of Chen Rui.
  • 楼氏 - The wife of Chen Tongfu.
  • 陇西李子平氏 - Refers to the Li family from Longxi, Gansu.
  • 茂德 - The son of Li Ziping.
  • 张氏 - The wife of Li Maode.
  • 仲德 - The younger brother of Li Maode.
  • 张氏 - The wife of Li Zhongde.
  • 希贤 - The brother of Li Ziping.
  • 陈氏 - The wife of Li Xixian.
  • 度 - The son of Li Xixian, who was born when his mother was pregnant.
  • 同知济南路总管府事 - A high-ranking official position during the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 陇西郡伯 - A noble title bestowed on Li Ziping's father.
  • 陇西郡夫人 - A noble title bestowed on Li Ziping's mother.

Proper Nouns

  • 奉化 - Fenghua, Zhejiang.
  • 陇西 - Longxi, Gansu.
  • 济南路 - Jinan Road, a region in the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 陇西郡 - Longxi County, a region in the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 同知 - A high-ranking official position.
  • 总管府事 - A high-ranking official position.
  • 陇西郡伯 - A noble title.
  • 陇西郡夫人 - A noble title.
  • 关襄宣慰阶中奉大夫 - A high-ranking official position.

[638]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 怪石林立 - Literally 'strange stones stand like a forest,' describing the appearance of the stones in the cave.
  • 上大其本小 - The top is large but the base is small, describing the shape of the stone.
  • 危立如种 - Standing precariously like a seed, suggesting the stone is unstable.
  • 自石上涌起 - Rising from the stone, describing the stone's emergence.
  • 稍加力排辄不动 - Adding a bit of force makes it immovable, showing the stone's resistance to strong shaking.
  • 頫瞰不能见底 - Looking down, the bottom cannot be seen, indicating the depth of the cave.
  • 投以石不应 - Throwing a stone into it produces no response, suggesting the cave's depth or the stone's inability to reach the bottom.
  • 以声呼则相答 - Calling out produces an echo, describing the acoustics of the cave.
  • 深窅不测 - Deep and unfathomable, emphasizing the cave's mysterious and profound nature.
  • 黏接纸缝法 - Method of sticking paper seams, referring to a technique for joining paper.
  • 古法用楮树汁飞麫白笈末三物调和如糊 - The ancient method uses the juice of the paper mulberry tree, wheat flour, and white glue powder mixed together like paste, explaining the ingredients and method.
  • 以之黏接纸缝永不脱解过如胶漆之坚 - Using this to stick paper seams, they never come apart, even more firmly than glue and lacquer, describing the effectiveness of the method.
  • 担雪填井 - After marriage, when managing household affairs, it is called carrying snow to fill a well, another metaphorical situation.
  • 投河奔井 - In the context of marriage, the various items of dowry and gifts that cannot be lacking are called throwing oneself into a river or well, another metaphorical expression.
  • 擂盘珠 - When servants first arrive, they are called 'lump of pearl' because they are active without being prompted.
  • 算盘珠 - After a while, they are called 'abacus beads' because they move when prompted.
  • 佛顶珠 - After a long time, they are called 'Buddha's crown pearl' because they remain still even when prompted.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄龙洞 - A specific location in Fuzhou, known for its natural beauty and cultural significance.
  • 吴兴郡 - An ancient name for a region in present-day Zhejiang Province, known for its historical and cultural importance.
  • 隆平寺 - A temple in Qinglong Town, known for its historical and religious significance.
  • 永光字绝照 - A monk known for his expertise in preserving ancient scriptures.
  • 王古心 - A historical figure known for his scholarly work and notes.
  • 观物斋 - A study or room where the author received visitors and conducted scholarly activities.
  • 青龙镇 - A town in present-day Shanghai, known for its historical and cultural significance.
  • 纳婢仆 - The practice of hiring servants, which was common in traditional Chinese households.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄龙洞 - A specific location in Fuzhou.
  • 吴兴郡 - An ancient name for a region in present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 隆平寺 - A temple in Qinglong Town.
  • 永光字绝照 - A monk known for his expertise in preserving ancient scriptures.
  • 王古心 - A historical figure known for his scholarly work and notes.
  • 观物斋 - A study or room where the author received visitors and conducted scholarly activities.
  • 青龙镇 - A town in present-day Shanghai.

[639]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一钱太守 - Title given to Duan Cong, a Ming dynasty official, for his integrity and modesty.
  • 举世传 - Famous throughout the world or well-known everywhere.
  • 遗庙 - Remains or ruins of a temple.
  • 仕路多能者 - Many capable officials in the officialdom.
  • 拣大钱 - Play on words, choosing larger coins, symbolizing integrity and not accepting bribes.
  • 全真教 - A Taoist sect founded by Wang Chongyang in the 12th century.
  • 倡全真教 - Advocated the Quanzhen School.
  • 纪实 - Record or account of events.
  • 金主亮 - Emperor Shizong of Jin (Wanyan Liang), a ruler of the Jin dynasty.
  • 吏员 - Low-ranking official or clerk.
  • 咸阳人 - Someone from Xianyang, a city in Shaanxi province.
  • 马邱刘和之 - Four founders of the Quanzhen School: Ma Yu, Qiu Chuji, Liu Chong, and Wang Chongyang.
  • 仕路 - Officialdom or the path of government service.
  • 御史 - Official responsible for oversight and inspection.
  • 不以家行 - Did not leave home or remained at home.
  • 独与母居 - Lived alone with his mother.
  • 转畧齐鲁境 - Moved through the territories of Qi and Lu (modern-day Shandong).
  • 匿草间 - Hid in the grass.
  • 仓卒 - In a hurry or suddenly.
  • 具棺敛 - To prepare a coffin and burial.
  • 聚石葬 - Buried with stones.
  • 郓地西 - Location west of Yuncheng (modern-day Shandong).
  • 胁以白刃 - Threatened with a sword.
  • 大恸 - Greatly wept or cried bitterly.
  • 母在此 - Mother is here.
  • 舍之 - Left him alone or did not harm him.
  • 遗以衣粮 - Left him clothes and food.
  • 邑刳于兵 - Town destroyed by soldiers.
  • 失墓所在 - Lost the location of the grave.
  • 榛莽 - Dense, overgrown vegetation.
  • 故衣尚存 - Old clothes still existed.
  • 克迁祔祖茔 - Finally moved and buried her with the ancestors.
  • 浙西宪佥 - High-ranking official in Zhejiang province.
  • 省之钱唐 - Visited Qiantang (modern-day Hangzhou).
  • 马孝子 - Ma Berjie, known for his filial piety.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 一钱太守庙 - Temple dedicated to Duan Cong, known for his integrity.
  • 绍兴钱清镇 - Town in Shaoxing, Zhejiang province.
  • 王叔能参政 - Political figure who wrote a poem about the temple.
  • 刘宠 - Historical figure known for his integrity and modesty.
  • 金主亮贞元元年 - First year of Emperor Shizong of Jin's reign.
  • 王中孚 - Low-ranking official who advocated the Quanzhen School.
  • 章宗泰和四年 - Fourth year of Emperor Zhangzong of Jin's reign.
  • 元学士 - Scholar of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 紫微观 - Taoist temple.
  • 邹县 - County in Shandong province.
  • 江南行台监察御史 - High-ranking official responsible for oversight and inspection in the southern region.
  • 齐鲁境 - Territories of Qi and Lu, modern-day Shandong province.
  • 郓城 - City in Shandong province.
  • 钱唐 - Modern-day Hangzhou, Zhejiang province.

Proper Nouns

  • 紹興 - Era name of the Southern Song; rendered as Shaoxing.

[640]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 截发自刭不殊 - Describes an extreme act of defiance: cutting off her hair and attempting self-strangulation but surviving.
  • 秘色窑器 - Refers to a special type of porcelain with a unique, precious quality, often translated as 'secret color' porcelain.
  • 袭故京遗制 - Means 'following the old capital's traditions,' referring to the continuation of imperial pottery-making techniques after the fall of the Northern Song dynasty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天台金沙里 - Refers to a specific location in Taizhou, Zhejiang province, where the story of Yang Zhenfu is set.
  • 钱氏 - Refers to the rulers of the Southern Tang dynasty, known for their patronage of the arts, including ceramics.
  • 定州白磁器 - Refers to white porcelain produced in Dingzhou, Hebei province, popular during the Song dynasty.
  • 汝窑 - One of the most famous kilns of the Song dynasty, known for producing high-quality celadon porcelain.
  • 政和间 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Huizong of Song (1111–1125), a period of significant artistic and cultural development.
  • 京师 - Refers to the capital city of the Northern Song dynasty, Bianjing (modern-day Kaifeng).
  • 修内司 - A government office responsible for managing the imperial kilns.

Proper Nouns

  • 杨伯瑞 - The name of the man whose wife, Yang Zhenfu, remained chaste after his death.
  • 钱氏 - The ruling family of the Southern Tang dynasty.
  • 定州 - A city in Hebei province known for its porcelain production.
  • 汝窑 - A kiln in Ruzhou, Henan province, famous for its celadon porcelain.
  • 政和 - The era name of Emperor Huizong of Song.
  • 京师 - The capital city of the Northern Song dynasty.
  • 修内司 - A government office responsible for managing the imperial kilns.

[641]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 上古无墨 - Refers to the absence of ink in ancient times.
  • 挺防漆而书 - Describes the use of bamboo slips and lacquer for writing.
  • 中古方以石磨汁 - Indicates the use of stone grinding to make ink during the middle period.
  • 延安石液 - Refers to the ink made from stones in Yan'an.
  • 魏晋时始有墨丸 - Marks the beginning of ink pellets during the Wei and Jin dynasties.
  • 漆烟松煤夹和为之所以 - Describes the mixture of lacquer smoke and pine soot to make ink.
  • 晋人多用凹心砚者 - Explains that people in the Jin dynasty used hollow-center inkstones to store the ink.
  • 螺子墨 - Refers to a type of ink pellet.
  • 唐高丽岁贡松烟墨 - Indicates that Goryeo (Korea) annually presented pine soot ink to the Tang dynasty.
  • 用多年老松烟和麋鹿胶造成 - Describes the process of making ink using old pine soot and deer antler glue.
  • 廷珪初名廷邽 - Notes that the original name of Tinggui was Tinggui.
  • 李廷珪墨 - Refers to the ink made by Li Tinggui.
  • 油烟入脑麝金箔谓之龙香剂 - Describes a type of ink made with lampblack, musk, and gold leaf, called 'Dragon Fragrance Agent.'
  • 元祐间潘谷墨 - Refers to the ink made by Pan Gu during the Yuanyou period.
  • 蜀中蒲大韶梁杲徐伯常及雪斋齐峯叶茂实翁彦卿等出世 - Lists several famous ink makers from Sichuan.
  • 中统至元以来各有所传 - Indicates that since the Zongtong and Yuan periods, there have been various transmissions of ink-making techniques.
  • 可以仿古 - Suggests that modern techniques can still emulate ancient methods.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 上古 - Refers to the early period before the invention of ink.
  • 中古 - Refers to the middle period, roughly the Han dynasty.
  • 魏晋 - Refers to the Wei and Jin dynasties.
  • 唐 - Refers to the Tang dynasty.
  • 高丽 - Refers to Goryeo (Korea).
  • 宋 - Refers to the Song dynasty.
  • 元祐 - Refers to the Yuanyou period of the Song dynasty.
  • 元 - Refers to the Yuan dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有襌下复下用木之异耳 - Indicates a slight difference in the use of wood for different types of footwear, likely referring to the construction of shoes or sandals.
  • 古人舄屦履至阶必脱 - Describes an ancient custom where people would remove their shoes or sandals when reaching the threshold of a house.
  • 唯着韤而入 - Refers to wearing socks or stockings instead of shoes inside.
  • 礼户外有二屦 - Indicates a ritual practice where two pairs of shoes were kept outside the house, one for indoor use and one for outdoor use.
  • 汉赐劒履上殿 - Refers to a historical practice in the Han dynasty where certain officials were granted the privilege of wearing shoes and carrying swords into the imperial hall.
  • 谏不行则纳履而去 - Describes a situation where if advice was not heeded, the advisor would put on their shoes and leave.
  • 纳结也 - Refers to the act of putting on shoes or sandals.
  • 言纳履则在外明矣 - Indicates that the act of putting on shoes is associated with being outside.
  • 是脱履而入者也 - Refers to the practice of removing shoes before entering a building.
  • 王乔入朝双舄化鳬先至 - A story about Wang Qiao, a figure from ancient times, who transformed his shoes into cranes and arrived at court ahead of others.
  • 古者堂上皆有席 - States that in ancient times, mats or rugs were placed on the floor of rooms.
  • 所以着韤为宜 - Suggests that wearing socks or stockings was appropriate on these mats.
  • 况韤又从韦乎 - Implies that socks or stockings were often made from leather.
  • 按乡饮酒云说屦揖让如初升堂防云凡堂上揖行礼不脱屦 - Refers to a ritual described in the *Rites of Zhou* where guests would exchange shoes and bow before entering the hall.
  • 坐则脱屦 - Indicates that shoes were removed when sitting down.
  • 由是观之凡宗庙堂室之间行礼亦必不脱屦矣 - Concludes that in all formal settings, including temples and halls, shoes were not removed during rituals.
  • 夫降而脱屦然后升坐礼也 - Describes a ritual where one would remove their shoes before ascending to sit.
  • 其后賔与主人酬酢之时皆在两阶之间又湏降而着屦复升于阶 - Refers to a later practice where guests and hosts would exchange shoes and ascend the steps during a drinking ceremony.
  • 酬酢之礼毕又降而脱屦复升于坐也 - Indicates that after the ceremony, shoes were removed again before sitting down.
  • 古人礼繁如此今何畧也 - Compares ancient and modern ritual practices.
